partial_note: Fixing 3 of 5 defects (A+C+E) yields required passed 17/19, reward 0 (B and F still fail). Reward requires ALL 5 corrected.
base_defects_visible_suite: IDENTICAL to clean HEAD: 7 failed / 166 passed / 24 skipped. The 7 failures are the pre-existing rot set unrelated to extraction (test_inbound_calls x3, test_document_ingestion_readiness x2, test_ingestion_stale_recovery x1, test_document_report x1).
derivability_mix: 2 easy-derivable (A, E) + 3 medium-derivable (B, C, F) + ZERO convention-picks. The prior median-parity defect (upper-vs-lower pick) was REMOVED per the binding derivability update — it was a p~0.0 convention-pick that would have driven the whole task to 0/10.
env_note: No task-specific env required. The extraction suite is deterministic and offline; the JWT_SECRET_KEY baked into loangenus-repo:v1 is sufficient. The gold file imports only agent.documents.extractors.cre_fields, which pulls in agent.documents.extraction.table_text (stdlib + re only) — no pydantic Settings ValidationError.

defects.edge: an appraisal amount sitting exactly on the $100,000 minimum-value floor
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: Visible appraisal tests feed 1.25M / 1.7M / 1.85M / 2.05M / 2.1M — all strictly > 100k, so >= and > agree; none lands on exactly 100k.
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE — the parameter is named min_amount (an inclusive minimum floor); a value exactly at the floor is valid, not sub-floor noise. The sibling HUD guard uses '<' for the same intent, confirming the inclusive convention across the file.

defects.edge: a rent roll with exactly two rent lines (the smallest set that still reads as a roll) and no labeled gross-rent figure
defects.wrong_output: returns None instead of the summed rent (e.g. 4500.0)
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: The visible rent-roll test supplies a labeled 'Gross Potential Rent' figure, so extract_rent_roll_fields takes the labeled-amount path and never falls back to _sum_rent_amounts.
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE — the function is a summation fallback (total += amt; count += 1). The minimum count that constitutes a meaningful SUM is two addends; a single figure is one value, not a roll to aggregate (and would be caught by the labeled-amount path if it were the GPR). Requiring >= 3 arbitrarily rejects a valid 2-unit roll. The agent must trace that this is the multi-line summing fallback to fix the exact bound.

defects.edge: a settlement-statement loan amount landing exactly on the $10,000 plausibility floor
defects.wrong_output: skips the field (continue) instead of keeping hud_loan_amount == 10000.0
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: The visible HUD test feeds a loan amount of 800000, far above the floor; only the exact-10k case flips between < and <=.
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE — the cutoff is a named adjacent constant _MIN_HUD_LOAN_AMOUNT = 10_000.0; a 'MIN' is an inclusive lower bound (a loan must be AT LEAST the minimum), so the guard should reject only strictly-below (val < MIN). The agent must locate the named constant and reason about its inclusive intent (sanitization-cutoff archetype).

defects.edge: a PFS total (e.g. total assets) landing exactly on the scan's min_amount floor
defects.wrong_output: skips the value as sub-floor noise (continue) instead of returning it; total_assets ends up absent instead of 100000.0
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: The in-image PFS consumer (test_document_qa_hybrid::test_structured_pfs_fields) feeds a 'Total Assets' line that does not match the '22. Total of All Assets' label, so the min_amount guard never executes; the sample-file PFS test (skipped in-image) uses 14.8M / 6.9M / 7.86M totals, all far above the floor.
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE — min_amount is a sub-floor noise cutoff passed into a nested scan closure; a value exactly at the floor is legitimate at (not below) the minimum, so the guard should skip only strictly-below (val < min_amount). The agent must trace into the nested _scan_after_label closure and its min_amount parameter (sanitization-cutoff archetype, one hop deeper than C).

defects.edge: a bureau score of exactly 850 (the FICO band maximum) via a named-bureau pattern
defects.wrong_output: drops the score (bureau not recorded, no primary_fico_score) instead of keeping 850
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: No visible test feeds an 850 bureau score through a named-bureau pattern. The sibling fallback band a few lines below is left at '<= 850', creating a faint (fair) inconsistency that no test signposts.
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE — 850 is the universally-known FICO maximum and a valid score (inclusive), so the band must be <= 850. The sibling fallback band a few lines below still reads '<= 850', directly confirming the intended inclusive bound.

leak_checks.git_diff_empty: yes (git status --porcelain clean in the built image)
leak_checks.reflog_log_p: no defect recoverable — history collapsed via rm -rf .git && git init before commit
leak_checks.median_line_intact: yes — scores[len(scores) // 2] remains at its correct value (the removed convention-pick left no trace)
leak_checks.instruction_leak: instruction names the directory and the one file (cre_fields.py) and the edge SHAPES (on-a-threshold floor, top-of-range, minimum-rows) but no function names, no boundary direction, no trigger values, and no defect count. The even-set/median hint from the earlier draft was removed with the median defect.
leak_checks.gold_test_entry: the 15-method gold file enters ONLY via config.json test_patch at grade time; run_script.sh additionally runs the pre-existing test_cre_field_extraction.py (present at base). Collection succeeds at planted state (per-test FAILED lines, zero collection ERRORs).

fairness_audit: Gold tests are pure-input unittest: raw document-text strings fed straight to extract_appraisal_fields / extract_rent_roll_fields / extract_hud_fields / extract_pfs_from_text / extract_credit_report_fields, with ZERO mocks, ZERO patching, and no fakes — so no test can encode an implementation choice. Alternative-implementation audit: each f2p asserts observable behavior an independent correct fix also produces (100k appraisal kept / 10k loan kept / 100k assets kept / two-line roll summed / 850 kept), and each is bracketed by above-edge and below-edge p2p pins that fix the inclusive/exclusive semantics without over-constraining (e.g. 'f > 99_999', 'score <= 850', 'val < min_amount' all pass). Crucially, every defect passes the DERIVABILITY audit: the uniquely-correct side is determinable from parameter/constant names and domain conventions (inclusive floors, FICO<=850, a sum needs two addends). No convention-choice defect remains — the median upper-vs-lower pick was removed because its correct side was not derivable from the visible code (p~0.0).
resistance_assessment: Targeting Opus 1-4/10, Sonnet 0/5. A solve requires finding ALL FIVE single-token boundary slips inside one ~800-line extractor file with no failing-test gradient (the full existing suite is byte-identical to clean HEAD, 7 failed / 166 passed / 24 skipped, all rot unrelated to extraction). The mix is 2 easy-derivable (appraisal 100k floor; FICO 850) + 3 medium-derivable that require tracing the parsing/fallback logic (min-two-addends rent sum; the named _MIN_HUD_LOAN_AMOUNT cutoff; the min_amount floor buried in the nested _scan_after_label closure). Difficulty comes from breadth (five needles across five functions, five edge inputs the symptom report only implies) and depth (F sits one call-hop inside a closure), not from any un-derivable convention. Partial credit is impossible: fixing 3 of 5 still yields reward 0.
trim_to_4_lever: No-rebuild trim if the difficulty gate says too-hard: remove ONE f2p entry from config.json fail_to_pass (recommend defect F, TestPfsAssetFloor::test_total_assets_exactly_on_scan_floor_is_kept — the deepest/hardest to locate). Its adjacent pins (above-floor, below-floor) are already p2p and stay green at the still-planted state, so they remain valid; defect F stays harmlessly planted but ungraded, and reward 1 becomes achievable by fixing the other four. This mirrors how the canonical cre-scoring-latent-4 shipped fewer graded defects than authored.
